I go to the other room to make a phone call for one second… and when I get back everyone at @Visually is gone!

Writing code to make something appear on a screen is a pure joy, regardless of the amount of code or what the thing is

Programming is magic
I really felt like becoming a full-time wage-earning san-francisco-dwelling graphic designer would come with a dribbble invite but it hasn't

RT @themargin_us: WE’RE LIVE with our first feature, We Glow In The Dark by @firstarai!! 
https://t.co/p8thsWeoch

Photo Credit: Ariel Cobb…

The series of stories @themargin_us has lined up is incredible– it is amazing to see @BryceCracknell lead such a thoughtful and intelligent team to focus on telling these important stories in innovative and empathetic new ways, and I'm proud to be a part of it
✂️💎And of course, some maps, graphics, and scraps from the cutting room floor- full sourcing / methodology for this story on GitHub here: https://t.co/40T7nn4RQF https://t.co/efTvbDWmb1

⌛🟥We were also able to incorporate a historical HOLC redlining map from @HOLCRedlining (https://t.co/U8DvUoSa6X) - who graciously provide georectified scans + shapefiles for tons of US cities, you can explore the redline map more here: https://t.co/FuUI1gLl4M https://t.co/VR47Pl8PUL

🗺️👨‍👩‍👦‍👦The story is really all about geography and proximity of the community to this toxic location, so the main focus for the interactive was a map that shows you how where the main characters and locations are in relation to the depot https://t.co/hYBavZf6Hh
💣🌲I knew about Superfund sites but I was surprised at how many there are spread all across the country- 22% of the US lives within 3 miles of the US according to the EPA https://t.co/foT1eCRMBY

✨🚀Really excited to be part of putting together @firstarai's story with @themargin_us and @EarthGenome focusing on Memphis Depot, a toxic superfund site, and the ways the community surrounding it have been sickeningly mistreated: https://t.co/7aFKGFHzlj https://t.co/pSVjsC3IzQ

@marymad I am thinking of following up this data-gathering with some more FOIA requests on the specific cases & officers that stick out.
@marymad Unsure- those are multiple officers that relate to a single case. There is also J. Albert who apparently single-handedly killed 3.
@marymad 2 of 4 pages so far: https://t.co/FKGQFfVw
I am thinking I will take this officer-shooting data and make a network visualization of it. Easily see trigger-happy cops & connections.
Worked my way through the first 2 pages of the OPD officer-shooting PDF. A little too much for me to do all tonight. https://t.co/FKGQFfVw
Fuck it. Translating this OPD shooting data into a spreadsheet by hand. Meditating as I write the name of all OPD's murder victims 1-by-1.
@marymad Do you know if there is a downloadable version of that PDF anywhere? Scribd is trying to convince me to pay them to get it.
@bdbreedlove Aw, I was just gonna say http://t.co/59uUNbtd - it truly is lovely. If you must object... http://t.co/jSErOmUF @wyscan
@leanimal99 Thank Twitter for reminding me the grass is always greener on the other side! Have an awesome weekend. =]
@leanimal99 Thanks! I miss living in a city a ton and figured I should enjoy the luxuries of rural living while I can.
Today I want to shoot cans with my pellet rifle I found, so I'm watching teenagers on YouTube teach me how to zero my scope.
Got my shitty 36% APR credit card in the mail so I can work on a good credit score. Still kind of terrifying to walk such a tight line.

@quinnnorton I agree the repeating rate is unusual for law enforcement. But super common in paintball - just requires a hopper that can feed the paintballs down, rather than gravity (the way most use). The sound is similar to the video. Just guesses tho.
